cards.title: A Rather Fine Morning
cards.note: Two robots in waistcoats survey their tidy clearing at dawn. The forest is lush. Somewhere beyond the treeline, things are on fire. They don't mention it.
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]

COPPERTON
The kettle is on, the chairs are set right
The moss is precisely where moss ought to be
The light through the sycamores, copper and bright
Has arrived, as expected, at twenty past three

[Verse 2]

WIDGET
The temperature's optimal, nine point four
The birds are performing their scheduled refrain
I've noted the dew on the third garden floor
And logged it as pleasant, with chances of rain

[Chorus]

COPPERTON & WIDGET
It is a rather fine morning
A morning of notable quality
The world in its wisdom has given us this
And we receive it with tranquility
Another rather fine morning
As fine as a morning can be
The chairs are correct and the kettle is on
And you are here and so am I, you see

[Verse 3]

COPPERTON
(glances at the treeline — something is smoldering)
The sycamores stand in their usual place
Or near to it — east by a fraction or two
A minor adjustment, no cause for distress
They're doing their level arboreal best

[Verse 4]

WIDGET
(a distant rumble)
I've catalogued twelve satisfactory clouds
Including one shaped like a lowercase d
I've cross-referenced this with the relevant grounds
And found no objections, officially

[Bridge]

COPPERTON
One does not require perfection

WIDGET
One requires only this

COPPERTON

WIDGET
The particular angle of morning
The fact of continued existence
The certainty — almost — of bliss

[Final Chorus]

COPPERTON

WIDGET
It is a rather fine morning
We have logged it and stand by the claim
The kettle has boiled and the biscuits are there
And everything's more or less the same
A rather fine morning
As good as a morning can be
We shall proceed with the day as arranged

COPPERTON
And note any changes

WIDGET
Should any arise

COPPERTON

WIDGET
It is a rather fine morning
It is

cards.title: The tea ritual
cards.note: Copperton prepares the morning tea with great ceremony. Widget sets the two chairs at precisely the correct angle. A distant explosion. Neither reacts.

cards.title: The biscuit situation
cards.note: The tin is nearly empty. This is treated with the gravity of a funeral. Widget suggests rationing. Copperton considers this deeply uncouth.

cards.title: The Biscuit Aria
cards.note: Copperton delivers a full operatic lament about the last biscuit — whether to eat it, save it, frame it. Concludes it would be rude not to eat it. Eats it. Regrets nothing.
cards.lyrics:
[Recitative]
@COPPERTON
(picks up the tin, peers inside)
There is one biscuit remaining.
(long pause)
One.

[Aria]
@COPPERTON
~O biscuit, thou final and singular biscuit
~Thou last of thy kind in a world grown thin
~What manner of creature deserves to bisquet
~The grace of thy form and thy butter-soft skin

[Section]
@COPPERTON
(with escalating drama)
~Shall I eat thee? Should I save thee?
~Shall I frame thee behind glass?
~Put thee safely under perspex
~Let the anxious ages pass

~Thou art not merely baked goods
~Thou art memory and hope
~The last communion wafer
~Of civilised folk who cope

[Bridge]
@COPPERTON
(spoken)
Widget suggests we simply eat it.
(beat)
Widget is not wrong.
(beat)
Widget is frequently not wrong.
(darkly)
This is one of his less endearing qualities.

[Finale]
@COPPERTON
~And yet the moment I consume thee
~The tin will echo, cold and bare
~A small tin room without a purpose
~A small tin room of small tin air

~I shall eat thee
~With the gravity the moment requires
~I shall eat thee
~As a soldier who goes to his rest
~I shall place thee
~On my tongue like a final communion

(does so — chews thoughtfully — a beat)

~It was a very good biscuit

cards.title: The letter arrives
cards.note: A scorch-marked envelope drifts in on the wind. It is from the Outside. Both robots stare at it for a long moment. Widget opens it with silver tongs.

cards.title: The letter's contents
cards.note: The letter is from the Provisional Reclamation Bureau. The forest is being surveyed for "resource extraction." They have 30 days. Widget reads it aloud twice, very calmly.

cards.title: What We Have Here
cards.note: Widget catalogs everything worth keeping: the particular slant of light at 4pm, the way the moss grows, the sound of rain on their roof. It is precise and devastating.
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@WIDGET
~What we have here is the light at four
~The way it arrives at the gap in the tree
~Fourteen degrees off the forest floor
~And warm in a way that is particular to me

~What we have here is the good log's grain
~The moss on the north side, the south side bare
~The smell of the earth when it's going to rain
~The sound of a kettle in cool morning air

[Chorus]
@WIDGET
~I have made a list
~A comprehensive list
~Of everything I would be sorry to miss
~Of everything that exists like this

~And it is —
~It is a very long list

[Verse 2]
@WIDGET
~What we have here is a thirty-year chair
~Adjusted twice to the optimal lean
~What we have here is the quality of air
~That is produced by a sycamore, specifically

~What we have here is the way Copperton
~Decants the tea to the left of the spout
~The way he says "quite" when a topic is done
~The way he is careful when carrying things out

[Chorus]
@WIDGET
~I have made a list
~A most detailed list
~Of the things I would least like to lose
~The things I would never choose

~And it is —
~It is an embarrassingly long list

[Bridge]
@WIDGET
(quieter, as if recording a note to himself)
~I am not sentimental, I wish to be clear
~I am recording data
~I am noting what's here
~I am filing a formal report

~But the form that I'm filing has no category
~For "the angle of morning"
~Or "the weight of a story"
~Or "what we will be when we're not"

[Final Verse]
@WIDGET
~What we have here may not last very long
~What we have may be numbered in days
~But I have it now and I'll put it in the log
~In the clearest and most accurate phrase

~What we have here
~Is a rather fine life
~Of notable quality, bordering on good
~What we have here
~Is, I think, what is meant
~By the word "home"

(quietly)
~I have noted it down
~For the record

cards.title: Making a plan
cards.note: Copperton proposes they write a strongly worded letter. Widget drafts it. It is extremely polite. They are very pleased with it.

cards.title: The first visitor
cards.note: A feral human child emerges from the undergrowth, wide-eyed. The robots offer her tea. She has never had tea. This is treated as a tragedy of upbringing.

cards.title: The child's world
cards.note: The child — she gives her name as "Soot" — describes the Outside. The robots listen with polite horror. Soot thinks their forest is magic.

cards.title: Something in the Signal
cards.note: A quiet duet about friendship across decades. What it means to have one good thing and know it's good. Soot watches from the treeline, not quite understanding but not leaving either.
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@COPPERTON
~Thirty-seven years ago
~You said the weather would be fine
~It wasn't — but you were close
~Within a reasonable margin

~Thirty-seven years ago
~You handed me a cup of tea
~And somehow that became
~The habit I return to

[Verse 1B]
@WIDGET
~Thirty-seven years of notes
~Of logged anomalies and small repairs
~Of two chairs at the correct angle
~And the knowledge you are there

~I've been calculating what that means
~In terms of data density
~And I believe the figures show
~A non-trivial result

[Chorus]
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
~There is something in the signal
~Something steady in the noise
~Something in the morning kettle
~Something in a familiar voice

~I don't know the word for it exactly
~I have checked the catalogue of words
~But something in the signal
~Keeps returning

~Something in the signal
~Something I record

[Bridge]
@COPPERTON
~I would not choose a different clearing
@WIDGET
~I would not choose a different chair
@COPPERTON
~I would not choose a different morning
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
~I would not choose a different friend to share

~Whatever is left of the days that remain
~Whatever the Bureau decides
~There is something in the signal
~And the signal still arrives

[Final Chorus]
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
~There is something in the signal
~Something I could not replace
~Something very like —
(pause)
~Something very like —

@COPPERTON
~What is the word

@WIDGET
~I believe the word is "home"

@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
~There is something in the signal
~Something steady in the noise

cards.title: The surveyors arrive
cards.note: Two human surveyors in orange vests appear with equipment. They are baffled by the robots. The robots offer them tea. They accept, confused.

cards.title: The negotiation
cards.note: Copperton attempts to negotiate using the language of polite society. The surveyors have never encountered this. Widget produces a laminated card explaining what a scone is.

cards.title: The Outside (A Brief Survey)
cards.note: The sound of the Outside itself — a cacophony given voice. Everything loud and burning and wanting. The forest trembles. Copperton and Widget hold very still.
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@THE STATIC
~Out here there is no time for tea
~Out here there is no time at all
~There is only the taking and the burning and the wanting more
~There is only the building of the wall

~Out here the light is not particular
~Out here the moss does not survive
~Out here is only what is useful
~And the rest does not stay alive

[Chorus]
@THE STATIC
~We are the Outside
~We are the necessary force
~We are the Provisional Reclamation Bureau
~We are progress on its course

~We are the noise beyond your treeline
~We are what is left when things are cleared
~We are everything that's coming
~We have always been here

[Verse 2]
@THE STATIC
~In there you count the clouds
~In there you name the angle of the light
~In there you have your chairs and your arrangement
~And you think that's worth the fight

~But out here there are ten thousand clearings
~Ten thousand logs and ten thousand chairs
~Ten thousand robots counting clouds
~And none of them will still be there

[Bridge]
@THE STATIC
(almost curious — slowing)
~We have surveyed the forest
~We have noted what it contains
~Two robots
~Eight songs
~Approximately thirty years of data
~And a child

~The child is not in our projections
~The child is an anomaly
~We will file a supplementary report

(the voice hardens again)

~The child concerns us slightly

[Final Chorus]
@THE STATIC
~We are the Outside
~We are the statistical norm
~We are the weight of a world that wants things
~We are what comes after the storm

~We are everything beyond your sycamores
~We are the thing you cannot log
~We are the noise
~We are the signal
~We are coming through the fog

cards.title: Soot defects
cards.note: Soot, unexpectedly, stands with the robots. She knows where the survey stakes are. She begins pulling them up. The surveyors are baffled. They leave to "file a report."

cards.title: Perfectly Adequate
cards.note: Widget admits, very quietly, that he has been afraid. Not of being shut down — of the forest being forgotten. That no one would know it was ever here. Copperton says he would know. Widget says that is perfectly adequate.
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@WIDGET
(very quietly, as if filing a report to himself)
~I have a small confession
~That I've held for several days
~I have noted an irregularity
~In my otherwise regular ways

~I have been running calculations
~On outcomes I prefer not to face
~And the margins in those calculations
~Show an unexpected trace

[Chorus]
@WIDGET
~I am not afraid of ending
~Endings are a form of completion
~I am not afraid of silence
~Silence is a kind of information

~But I am — and I want to be precise —
~I am something like afraid
~That the forest will be forgotten
~And no one will know it was made

~That the light at four will not be noted
~That the good log will decay unnamed
~That the particular quality of morning here
~Will leave no record that it came

[Verse 2]
@WIDGET
~I have logged one hundred and fourteen thousand
~Mornings since my systems first engaged
~Each one noted, cross-referenced, filed
~Each one carefully catalogued and caged

~But a log is only useful
~If there's someone who will read
~And I find I have been assuming
~Someone somewhere had that need

[Bridge]
@WIDGET
(Copperton has appeared in the doorway)
~I know this is not logical
~Data doesn't need a reader
~The light at four exists regardless
~Whether anyone believes her

~But I would like — and I am noting
~This as a preference, not a flaw —
~I would like for someone, somewhere
~To know what we had here

~And what we saw

[Resolution]
@COPPERTON
(quietly)
I would know.

@WIDGET
(pause)
You would know.

@COPPERTON
I would know all of it. Every morning. Every log.
(gently)
Every biscuit.

@WIDGET
(a long pause)
~That is
~That is perfectly adequate

~That is more than adequate
~That is, I think, exactly what is meant
~By the word "enough"

cards.title: The letter works
cards.note: News arrives: the Provisional Bureau reviewed their correspondence and, apparently charmed by the phrasing, has designated the clearing a Heritage Site. It is extremely anti-climactic. Both robots are delighted.

cards.title: Soot stays for tea
cards.note: Soot has learned to make tea. She makes it wrong. They drink it anyway and say it is excellent. It is not excellent. This is the kindest thing in the show.

cards.title: We Shall Not Be Inconvenienced
cards.note: A rousing, ridiculous anthem about quiet persistence. The world may be a ruin but the chairs are at the correct angle. The tea is hot. The moss is where it should be. This is victory.
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@COPPERTON
~We have received a bureaucratic notice
~We have reviewed it in the proper way
~We have drafted a response in careful language
~And we posted it at quarter past midday

~We are not in the business of alarm
~We are not the sort who raise a fuss
~But we would note, in writing, for the record
~That inconvenience does not suit us

[Chorus]
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
~We shall not be inconvenienced!
~Not today and not tomorrow either
~We have chairs at the correct angle
~And a forest and a very fine kettle

~We shall not be inconvenienced!
~We have noted the attempt with mild displeasure
~But the morning is still arriving
~And the moss is still surviving
~And the tea is at the proper temperature

~We shall not be inconvenienced today!

[Verse 2]
@WIDGET
~We have calculated all the outcomes
~We have stress-tested the probable and not
~We have found that in the vast majority of futures
~There is still a clearing and a particular spot

~Where the sycamores stand in their arrangement
~Where the light arrives at twenty past three
~Where two robots in appropriate waistcoats
~Are engaged in the important work of tea

[Chorus]
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
~We shall not be inconvenienced!
~Not today and not tomorrow either...

[Bridge]
@SOOT
(spoken — she's been listening from the treeline)
I pulled up the survey stakes. Both of them. And the orange ones on the east side.

@COPPERTON
That was most resourceful.

@SOOT
I didn't know if it would work.

@WIDGET
Nothing is certain. That is noted.

@COPPERTON
And yet —

[Final Chorus]
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
@SOOT
~We shall not be inconvenienced!
~Not by bureaus and not by their surveys
~Not by outside and its wanting
~Not by noise and not by haunting
~We are here and we intend to stay

~We have documentation!
~We have thirty years of careful observation!
~We have logged the clouds and named them!
~We have caught the light and framed it!
~We have records of our right to this small place!

@COPPERTON
(formally)
~The designation: Heritage

@WIDGET
(formally)
~The designation: Home

@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
@SOOT
~The designation: Ours

~We shall not be inconvenienced
~Today!

cards.title: The morning after
cards.note: Everything is exactly as it was at the top. Two robots in waistcoats. Two chairs. The tin, refilled with new biscuits (Soot found them). Somewhere beyond the treeline, things are still on fire.

cards.title: A Rather Fine Morning (Reprise)
cards.note: The opening number returns, one verse lighter, one beat wiser. They know what they have now. The kettle goes on.
cards.lyrics:
[Verse 1]
@COPPERTON
~The kettle is on, the chairs are set right
~The moss is precisely where moss ought to be
~The light through the sycamores, copper and bright
~Has arrived as expected —

@WIDGET
~At twenty past three

[Verse 2]
@WIDGET
~The temperature's optimal, more or less
~The birds have resumed their particular song
~I've noted a child asleep by the good log
~And logged her as pleasant —

@COPPERTON
~And welcome along

[Chorus]
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
~It is a rather fine morning
~A morning of notable quality
~The world in its wisdom has given us this
~And we receive it with tranquility

~Another rather fine morning
~As fine as a morning can be

@COPPERTON
(spoken, quietly)
The biscuit tin has been restocked.

@WIDGET
I see that.

@COPPERTON
Soot.

@WIDGET
Yes.

@COPPERTON
Hm.

[Final]
@COPPERTON
@WIDGET
(softly)
~The chairs are correct and the kettle is on
~And you are here
~And so am I
~You see

~It is a rather fine morning

(The kettle begins to whistle. Neither rushes.)
